tool_args.label: System-Wide Platform Screen Door Feasibility Study Summary of Conclusions
tool_args.raw_text: System-Wide Platform Screen Door Feasibility Study Summary of Conclusions. February 2020 STV Inc. 225 Park Avenue South New York

payload.title: System-Wide Platform Screen Door Feasibility Study Summary of Conclusions
payload.description: Feasibility study of installing platform screen doors (PSDs), automatic platform gates (APGs), and rope platform screen doors (RPSDs) at all 472 NYCT subway stations. Assesses technical, structural, and operational feasibility with cost estimates.

evidence_refs.source_quote: New York City Transit SYSTEM-WIDE PLATFORM SCREEN DOOR FEASIBILITY STUDY SUMMARY OF CONCLUSIONS
February 2020 STV Inc. 225 Park Avenue South New York

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: System-Wide Platform Screen Door Feasibility Study
tool_args.raw_text: System-wide PSD Feasibility Study

payload.project_name: System-Wide Platform Screen Door Feasibility Study
payload.description: Study of all 472 NYCT subway stations to determine feasibility of installing platform screen doors (PSDs), automatic platform gates (APGs), and/or rope platform screen doors (RPSDs)

evidence_refs.source_quote: New York City Transit SYSTEM-WIDE PLATFORM SCREEN DOOR FEASIBILITY STUDY SUMMARY OF CONCLUSIONS
In March 2017 STV began the study of all 472 subway stations

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: System-wide PSD feasibility percentage
tool_args.raw_text: Overall system-wide feasibility is 27% , or 128 of the 472 stations studied.

payload.description: Percentage of all 472 NYCT subway stations found feasible for PSD/APG installation

evidence_refs.source_quote: Overall system-wide feasibility is 27% , or 128 of the 472 stations studied.

tool_args.label: Feasible stations today (current door alignment)
tool_args.raw_text: today, due to door misalignments, PSDs could only be implemented at 41 of the 128 stations

payload.scope: current conditions with existing rolling stock
payload.description: Stations where PSDs could be implemented today given current car door alignment profiles

evidence_refs.source_quote: today, due to door misalignments, PSDs could only be implemented at 41 of the 128 stations

tool_args.label: Total PSD (full-height) system-wide cost
tool_args.raw_text: The PDS (full height) option totals $7.01B

payload.description: Total rough order of magnitude (ROM) cost for PSD (full height) option including design, construction, Transit Authority Labor and Engineering Force Account costs

evidence_refs.source_quote: The PDS (full height) option totals $7.01B and the APG (half height) option totals $6.53B

tool_args.raw_text: the APG (half height) option totals $6.53B

payload.description: Total rough order of magnitude (ROM) cost for APG (half height) option

evidence_refs.source_quote: the APG (half height) option totals $6.53B

tool_args.raw_text: approximate cumulative annual maintenance costs of $119.16M for either

payload.description: Approximate cumulative annual maintenance cost for either PSD or APG option

evidence_refs.source_quote: approximate cumulative annual maintenance costs of $119.16M for either

tool_args.label: Study covered all 472 NYCT stations
tool_args.raw_text: STV began the study of all 472 subway stations to determine the feasibility of installing fixed railings, PSDs, APGs, and/or RPSDs at each station (nominally 472 stations) and every platform (more than 1200 platform edges).

payload.claim_text: STV studied all 472 NYCT subway stations and more than 1200 platform edges to determine PSD/APG/RPSD feasibility
payload.subject: System-Wide Platform Screen Door Feasibility Study
payload.description: Scope of the feasibility study

evidence_refs.source_quote: STV began the study of all 472 subway stations to determine the feasibility of installing fixed railings, PSDs, APGs, and/or RPSDs at each station (nominally 472 stations) and every platform (more than 1200 platform edges).

tool_args.label: Car door misalignment issue
tool_args.raw_text: Currently the NYCT Subway system features cars with three different door alignment profiles on the A Division and three on the B Division. The different spacing of doors on these cars makes installation of platform doors infeasible at most stations today.

payload.claim_text: The NYCT Subway system has 3 different door alignment profiles on A Division and 3 on B Division, making PSD installation infeasible at most stations today. NYCT plans homogenized car classes by 2033.
payload.subject: rolling stock door alignment
payload.description: Car door alignment diversity prevents PSD installation at most stations currently

evidence_refs.source_quote: Currently the NYCT Subway system features cars with three different door alignment profiles on the A Division and three on the B Division. The different spacing of doors on these cars makes installation of platform doors infeasible at most stations today.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: NYCT plans homogenized rolling stock by 2033
tool_args.raw_text: NYCT plans to complete the procurement of new rolling stock, per division, with nearly identical car geometries/door spacing, by 2033. Accordingly, STV's assessment assumes homogenized car classes, per division, by the year 2033.

payload.claim_text: NYCT plans to complete procurement of new rolling stock with nearly identical car geometries/door spacing per division by 2033, enabling PSD feasibility at remaining stations
payload.subject: rolling stock procurement
payload.description: New rolling stock procurement timeline enabling PSD installations

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYCT plans to complete the procurement of new rolling stock, per division, with nearly identical car geometries/door spacing, by 2033.

tool_args.label: Fixed railings and RPSDs eliminated early
tool_args.raw_text: Fixed railings were eliminated early in the study due to expressed concerns by NYCT OSS and MOW regarding the potential injury they could cause to customers due to door dragging incidents. RPSDs were also assessed and removed due to their very limited application world-wide, boarding and alighting issues, and other hazards they posed to customers.

payload.claim_text: Fixed railings were eliminated due to door dragging injury concerns. RPSDs were eliminated due to limited worldwide application, boarding/alighting issues, and customer hazards.
payload.subject: barrier technology assessment
payload.description: Elimination of fixed railings and RPSD technologies from study

evidence_refs.source_quote: Fixed railings were eliminated early in the study due to expressed concerns by NYCT OSS and MOW regarding the potential injury they could cause to customers due to door dragging incidents. RPSDs were also assessed and removed due to their very limited application world-wide, boarding and alighting issues, and other hazards they posed to customers.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: Cost estimate basis
tool_args.raw_text: All cost estimates are based on 2018-19 dollars. Escalation costs out to possible year of award are not included and are expected to be around 4% per annum.

payload.claim_text: All cost estimates are based on 2018-19 dollars. Escalation (estimated at ~4% per annum) not included.
payload.subject: cost estimation methodology
payload.description: Basis year and escalation assumptions for cost estimates

evidence_refs.source_quote: All cost estimates are based on 2018-19 dollars. Escalation costs out to possible year of award are not included and are expected to be around 4% per annum.

tool_args.label: PSD requires redesign of refuse removal process
tool_args.raw_text: the implementation of a PSD system will likely require a re-design of the refuse removal process

payload.claim_text: Implementation of a PSD system will likely require a re-design of the refuse removal process (garbage train) due to varying stop positions and potential damage from metal garbage carts.
payload.subject: refuse removal process
payload.description: Operational impact of PSD on garbage train operations

evidence_refs.source_quote: the implementation of a PSD system will likely require a re-design of the refuse removal process

tool_args.label: Car door misalignment issue
tool_args.raw_text: Currently the NYCT Subway system features cars with three different door alignment profiles on the A Division and three on the B Division. The different spacing of doors on these cars makes installation of platform doors infeasible at most stations today.

payload.claim_text: The NYCT Subway system has 3 different door alignment profiles on A Division and 3 on B Division, making PSD installation infeasible at most stations today. NYCT plans homogenized car classes by 2033.

payload.description: Car door alignment diversity prevents PSD installation at most stations currently

RPSDs were also assessed and removed due to their very limited application world-wide, boarding and alighting issues, and other hazards they posed to customers.
